blurb:
Yard House at The Battery is a good call when you're already heading to a Braves game or want to make a night of it in that area. The menu is big, the beer list is huge, and it has a sports bar feel without being a dive. It's the kind of place where everyone in your group will find something they want.

The food is New American, so expect things like burgers, flatbreads, and a wide range of appetizers alongside whatever's on draft. The draft beer selection alone is worth a mention if you're into that sort of thing.

It stays open late on Fridays and Saturdays (until 12:30am), so it works well as a post-game spot too. Expect a wait on game days.
address: 825 Battery Ave SE #320, Atlanta, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
Pappasito's is the kind of Tex-Mex spot that's easy to suggest when you've got a group and nobody can agree on anything. The place has a fun, festive atmosphere and the margaritas are legitimately good. It gets busy on weekends, but it moves well for a bigger restaurant.

The Tex-Mex dishes are the reason people keep coming back. If you haven't been, the fajitas and margaritas are where to start. It works great for a family dinner or a night out with friends when you want something a little more lively than your average weeknight spot.

It's right off Windy Hill, so parking and access are easy. A solid go-to when you want a reliable, crowd-pleasing meal.
address: 2788 Windy Hill Rd SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

blurb:
If you've got kids who need to burn some energy or you're planning a birthday party that doesn't require much coordination on your end, Dave and Buster's gets the job done. It's loud and busy, but that's kind of the point. The arcade is the main draw, and the food is solid sports-bar fare that works fine alongside the chaos.

It's not a place you go for a quiet dinner, but it's great for a group outing where everyone has different ideas about what they want to do. Kids are happy with the games, adults can catch a game on one of the many screens and grab a drink.

Fridays and Saturdays it stays open until 2am, so it works for older kids and adults looking for a casual night out too.
address: 2215 D and B Dr SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

blurb:
Derek's is one of those places you're glad you know about. It's a casual breakfast and lunch spot on Canton Road, but what keeps people coming back is the wings. The honey hot wings in particular have a real following, and people talk about the fish and shrimp fried rice like it's a secret worth keeping.

Everything comes out hot and made to order, which you don't always get at a spot this affordable. It's good for a weekday breakfast, a quick lunch, or honestly a late-night run since they're open until 5am.

Just note the hours are a little unusual. They close in the afternoon between 2:30 and 4pm, so plan around that gap if you're going for a late lunch.
address: 1779 Canton Rd NE, Marietta, GA 30066, USA
